zh.lifeSummary: 當John Alexander Webb I於1405年6月5日出生在英國瓦維其可爾沃里克時，他的父親Geoffrey Webb33歲，母親Julia Mary de Burton33歲。他於1424年3月16日與Johanna Hall在美國堪薩斯州薩姆納倫敦結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1455年在他的家鄉享年50歲，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾。

nameMeanings.name: Webb
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a weaver, from early Middle English webbe (Old English webba (masculine) or webbe (feminine), probably used of both male and female weavers). This word survived into Middle English long enough to give rise to the surname, but was already obsolescent as an agent noun; hence the secondary forms with the agent suffixes -er and -ster (see Webster , Webber and compare Weaver ).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, cognates of 1, including Weber and Weberman.
History: Richard Webb, a Lowland Scot, was an admitted freeman of Boston in 1632, and in 1635 was one of the first settlers of Hartford, CT.

variants.name: Webster
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webbester ‘weaver’ (Old English webbestre ‘female weaver’). By the time of surname formation, the gender distinction of the -stre suffix had almost completely disappeared. Compare Webb , Webber , and Weaver .

History: The name Webster was brought to North America from England independently by several different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One John Webster settled in Ipswich, MA, in 1635; another John Webster (died 1661), ancestor of the lexicographer Noah Webster, emigrated to Cambridge, MA, c. 1631 and later became one of the founders of the colony of CT, of which he was appointed governor in 1656. Daniel Webster (1782–1852), politician and orator, was born in Salisbury, NH, a descendant of Thomas Webster, a prominent 17th-century citizen of Ipswich, MA, whose family had settled there around 1635, while he was still a child.

variants.name: Webber
meanings: English (Devon and Somerset): occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webber, an agent derivative of Webb .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Weber   ‘weaver’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John Alexander Webb I was born on 5 June 1405, in Warwick, Warwickshire, England, his father, Geoffrey Webb, was 33 and his mother, Julia Mary de Burton, was 33. He married Johanna Hall on 16 March 1424, in London, Sumner, Kansas, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1455, in his hometown, at the age of 50, and was buried in Warwickshire, England.

Il a épousé Johanna Hall le 16 mars 1424, à London, Sumner, Kansas, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1455, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Warwickshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: John Alexander Webb Iが1405年6月5日にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックで生まれた時，父Geoffrey Webbは33歳，母Julia Mary de Burtonは33歳であった。1424年3月16日にアメリカ合衆国カンザス州スムナーロンドンでJohanna Hallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1455年に故郷で50歳で死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Alexander Webb I(이)가 1405년 6월 5일 영국 워윅실르 워윅에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffrey Webb(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Julia Mary de Burton(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1424년 3월 16일 미국 캔자스 주 섬너 런던에서 Johanna Hall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1455년 그의 고향에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르에 묻혔다.

ru.Stratford-upon-Avon, Warwickshire, Engla: стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Alexander Webb I родился 5 июня 1405 г. в местности варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Geoffrey Webb было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Julia Mary de Burton, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Johanna Hall 16 марта 1424 г. в местности лондон, сумнир, Канзас, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1455 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности варвиккшир, Англия.
